question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect node routing by injecting a crafted value into the HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES environment variable (e.g., pointing to an attacker-controlled IP:port with a valid account ID format), so that LoadConfig() populates mirrorConfig.Rosetta.Nodes with adversarial endpoints, causing transactions submitted via the Rosetta construction API to be routed to wrong nodes and never exported to the real mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value with a duplicate IP:port key (e.g., '192.168.0.1:50211:0.0.3,192.168.0.1:50211:0.0.4') so that nodeInfoMap silently overwrites the first entry with the second, causing the NodeMap to reference an incorrect node account ID and resulting in missing or misattributed records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value with extra whitespace or Unicode spaces around the colon delimiter (e.g., ' 192.168.0.1 : 50211 : 0.0.3') so that strings.TrimSpace() only trims the outer node string but not the parts, causing fmt.Sprintf('%s:%s', parts[0], parts[1]) to produce a malformed key that never matches any real node endpoint, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value with an empty account ID segment (e.g., '192.168.0.1:50211:') so that hiero.AccountIDFromString('') either returns a zero-value AccountID or an error, causing nodeMapDecodeHookFunc to silently map the endpoint to account 0.0.0, leading to incorrect node-to-account mapping and incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value with an extremely large number of comma-separated node entries (e.g., 100,000 entries) so that the loop in loadNodeMapFromEnv() consumes excessive memory building nodeInfoMap, causing the process to OOM-crash before LoadConfig() returns, resulting in the mirror node exporting zero records during the downtime?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that v.AutomaticEnv() is called after config files are loaded, by setting environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to an unrecognized network name (e.g., 'FAKENET'), so that strings.ToLower() normalizes it to 'fakenet' and the Rosetta asserter rejects all network-specific requests, causing all block and transaction records to be inaccessible and effectively missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set the environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_ONLINE to 'false' (overriding the default via v.AutomaticEnv()), so that the server starts in offline mode and all data-retrieval endpoints (block, account, network) return errors, causing all records that should be exported to the mirror node to be inaccessible to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK to '0' via environment variable override through v.AutomaticEnv(), so that the BlockAPIService is initialized with MaxTransactionsInBlock=0, causing every block response to contain zero transactions and all transaction records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK to '1' via environment variable, so that only one transaction per block is exported, causing all remaining transactions in multi-transaction blocks to be silently truncated and missing from the mirror node's exported records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEREFRESHINTERVAL to an extremely large duration (e.g., '87600h' = 10 years) via environment variable, so that the address book is never refreshed, causing the NodeMap to remain stale with outdated or decommissioned node endpoints, resulting in transactions being routed to non-existent nodes and records never being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEREFRESHINTERVAL to '1ns' via environment variable, so that the address book refresh fires continuously, causing excessive database load and potential race conditions in NodeMap updates, leading to intermittent incorrect node routing and missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path pointing to a world-readable file they control (e.g., '/tmp/evil.yml') containing a crafted YAML that overrides hiero.mirror.rosetta.nodes with attacker-controlled endpoints, so that mergeExternalConfigFile() merges the malicious config and LoadConfig() populates NodeMap with adversarial nodes, causing records to be routed to wrong nodes and missing from the legitimate mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path of a very large YAML file (e.g., gigabytes) so that v.MergeInConfig() reads the entire file into memory, causing OOM or extreme latency in LoadConfig(), resulting in the mirror node failing to start and exporting zero records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file containing a deeply nested structure that causes viper's recursive merge to enter an exponential processing path, so that mergeExternalConfigFile() hangs indefinitely, preventing LoadConfig() from completing and causing the mirror node to export no records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to an empty string, so that strings.ToLower('') produces '' and the NetworkIdentifier is set to an empty network name, causing the Rosetta asserter to reject all requests and all records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_STATEMENTTIMEOUT to '0' via environment variable, so that Db.StatementTimeout is set to zero (no timeout), allowing long-running queries to hold database connections indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool and causing all subsequent record-export queries to fail with connection errors, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_POOL_MAXOPENCONNECTIONS to '1' via environment variable, so that Pool.MaxOpenConnections is set to 1, causing all concurrent record-export queries to serialize and time out under load, resulting in missing or delayed records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_POOL_MAXOPENCONNECTIONS to '0' via environment variable, so that Pool.MaxOpenConnections is set to 0 (unlimited), causing the database to be overwhelmed with connections and crash, resulting in all record-export queries failing and missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_HOST to an attacker-controlled database host via environment variable override through v.AutomaticEnv(), so that Db.Host points to a malicious server that returns fabricated records, causing the mirror node to export incorrect transaction and balance records to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_PORT to '0' via environment variable, so that Db.Port is set to 0 and GetDsn() produces a DSN with 'port=0', causing all database connections to fail and all record-export queries to return errors, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_CACHE_ENTITY_MAXSIZE to '0' via environment variable, so that Cache.MaxSize for the entity cache is set to 0, disabling entity caching entirely and causing every block request to re-query the database for entity data, leading to excessive load and potential timeouts that result in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_CACHE_ENTITY_MAXSIZE to a negative integer via environment variable, so that the cache is initialized with a negative max size, causing a panic or undefined behavior in the cache library, crashing the server and resulting in zero records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_SHARD to an incorrect shard value (e.g., '999') via environment variable, so that mirrorConfig.Common.Shard is set to 999, causing all entity ID resolution (account, token, contract) to use the wrong shard prefix, resulting in incorrect records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_REALM to an incorrect realm value (e.g., '999') via environment variable, so that mirrorConfig.Common.Realm is set to 999, causing all entity ID resolution to use the wrong realm prefix, resulting in incorrect or missing records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEVERSION to an arbitrary string (e.g., 'FAKE_VERSION') via environment variable, so that the Version struct's NodeVersion field is set to a fabricated value, causing the /network/status endpoint to report incorrect node version information to downstream consumers relying on it for record validation?
